//  NavigationManager.swift
//  VSCodeiPadOS
//
//  Go to Definition support with Cmd+Click and F12
//  FEAT-134: Symbol table tracking and navigation

import Foundation
import SwiftUI
#if canImport(UIKit)
import UIKit
#endif

// MARK: - Symbol Location Model

/// Represents a location in a source file
struct SymbolLocation: Identifiable, Equatable, Hashable {
    let id = UUID()
    let file: String           // File path or identifier
    let fileName: String       // Display name
    let line: Int              // 1-based line number
    let column: Int            // 1-based column number
    let endLine: Int?          // Optional end line for range
    let endColumn: Int?        // Optional end column for range

    init(file: String, line: Int, column: Int = 1, endLine: Int? = nil, endColumn: Int? = nil) {
        self.file = file
        self.fileName = (file as NSString).lastPathComponent
        self.line = line
        self.column = column
        self.endLine = endLine
        self.endColumn = endColumn
    }

    var displayString: String {
        "\(fileName):\(line):\(column)"
    }
}

// MARK: - Symbol Definition Model

/// Represents a symbol definition with its location and metadata
struct SymbolDefinition: Identifiable, Equatable {
    let id = UUID()
    let name: String                  // Symbol name
    let kind: SymbolKind              // Type of symbol
    let location: SymbolLocation      // Where it's defined
    let signature: String?            // Optional function signature or type info
    let documentation: String?        // Optional doc comment
    let containerName: String?        // Parent class/struct/module

    static func == (lhs: SymbolDefinition, rhs: SymbolDefinition) -> Bool {
        lhs.name == rhs.name && lhs.location == rhs.location
    }
}

// MARK: - Symbol Kind

enum SymbolKind: String, CaseIterable {
    case function = "function"
---END PREVIEW---

// MARK: - Output Types

/// Stream type for output lines
enum StreamType {
    case stdout
    case stderr
}

/// Output channel categories
enum OutputChannel: String, CaseIterable, Identifiable {
    case output = "Output"
    case javascript = "JavaScript"
    case python = "Python"
    case remote = "Remote"
    case debug = "Debug"

        case .remote: return "cloud"
        case .debug: return "ant.circle"

        case .output: return .primary
        case .javascript: return .yellow
        case .python: return .blue
        case .remote: return .purple
        case .debug: return .orange
        }
    }
}

    let text: String

=== RecentFileManager.swift === (      43 lines)
import Foundation

class RecentFileManager: ObservableObject {
    static let shared = RecentFileManager()
    
    @Published var recentFiles: [URL] = []
    private let maxRecentFiles = 10
    private let defaultsKey = "recentFileURLs"
    
    private init() {
        loadRecentFiles()
    }
    
    func addRecentFile(_ url: URL) {
        // Remove existing if present to move it to top
        recentFiles.removeAll { $0 == url }
        
        // Add to front
        recentFiles.insert(url, at: 0)
        
        // Trim
        if recentFiles.count > maxRecentFiles {
            recentFiles = Array(recentFiles.prefix(maxRecentFiles))
        }
        
        saveRecentFiles()
    }
    
    private func saveRecentFiles() {
        let urlStrings = recentFiles.map { $0.absoluteString }
        UserDefaults.standard.set(urlStrings, forKey: defaultsKey)
    }
    
    private func loadRecentFiles() {
        guard let urlStrings = UserDefaults.standard.stringArray(forKey: defaultsKey) else { return }
        recentFiles = urlStrings.compactMap { URL(string: $0) }
    }
    
    func clearRecents() {
        recentFiles.removeAll()
        UserDefaults.standard.removeObject(forKey: defaultsKey)
    }
}

//
//  RemoteRunner.swift
//  VSCodeiPadOS
//
//  Remote code execution via SSH with streaming output support
//

import Foundation
import SwiftUI
import NIO
import NIOSSH
import NIOCore
import Combine

// MARK: - SSH Command Output Types

enum SSHCommandOutput: Sendable {
    case stdout(String)
    case stderr(String)
    case exit(Int32)
    case timeout
    case error(Error)
}

struct SSHCommandResult: Sendable {
    let stdout: String
    let stderr: String
    let exitCode: Int
    let isTimedOut: Bool
}

extension SSHClientError {
    static let commandExecutionFailed: (String) -> SSHClientError = { reason in
        return .connectionFailed("Command execution failed: \(reason)")
    }
}

// MARK: - Process Model for Remote Execution

struct RemoteProcess: Identifiable, Equatable, Sendable {
    let id: UUID = UUID()
    let command: String
    let pid: Int?
    let startTime: Date
    var exitCode: Int?
    var endTime: Date?
    
    static func == (lhs: RemoteProcess, rhs: RemoteProcess) -> Bool {
        lhs.id == rhs.id
    }
    
    var duration: TimeInterval {
        let end = endTime ?? Date()
        return end.timeIntervalSince(startTime)
    }
    
    var isRunning: Bool {
        exitCode == nil
    }
}

import Foundation
import JavaScriptCore

// MARK: - Supporting Types

/// Supported programming languages for execution
public enum Language: String, CaseIterable, Sendable {
    case javascript = "javascript"
    case python = "python"
    case swift = "swift"
    case typescript = "typescript"
    case bash = "bash"
    case ruby = "ruby"
    case php = "php"
    case go = "go"
    case rust = "rust"
    case lua = "lua"
    case wasm = "wasm"
    case unknown = "unknown"
    
    /// Returns true if the language can potentially run on-device
    public var supportsOnDeviceExecution: Bool {
        switch self {
        case .javascript, .lua:
            return true // JavaScriptCore available
        case .python:
            return false // Requires embedded Python or Pyodide
        case .swift:
            return true // Can run in limited sandbox
        case .typescript:
            return true // Transpiles to JS
        case .wasm:
            return true // WebAssembly via WKWebView
        case .bash, .ruby, .php, .go, .rust, .unknown:
            return false // Requires interpreter/compiler not on iOS
        }
    }
    
    /// File extensions for this language
    public var fileExtensions: [String] {
        switch self {
        case .javascript: return ["js", "mjs"]
        case .python: return ["py", "pyw"]
        case .swift: return ["swift"]
        case .typescript: return ["ts", "tsx"]
        case .bash: return ["sh", "bash"]
        case .ruby: return ["rb"]
        case .php: return ["php"]
        case .go: return ["go"]
        case .rust: return ["rs"]
        case .lua: return ["lua"]
        case .wasm: return ["wasm"]
        case .unknown: return []
        }
    }

    public static func detect(from filename: String, code: String? = nil) -> Language {

        willSet { objectWillChange.send() }
    }

        case off
        case afterDelay
        case onFocusChange
        case onWindowChange
    }

import Foundation
import CoreSpotlight
import MobileCoreServices
import UniformTypeIdentifiers

/// Manages CoreSpotlight indexing for opened files
class SpotlightManager {
    static let shared = SpotlightManager()
    
    private let searchableIndex = CSSearchableIndex.default()
    private let domainIdentifier = "com.vscode.ipados.files"
    
    private init() {}
    
    // MARK: - Public API
    
    /// Index a file that was opened in the editor
    /// - Parameters:
    ///   - url: The file URL
    ///   - content: The file content
    ///   - fileName: The file name
    func indexFile(url: URL, content: String, fileName: String) {
        let attributeSet = CSSearchableItemAttributeSet(contentType: .content)
        
        // Basic attributes
        attributeSet.title = fileName
        attributeSet.displayName = fileName
        attributeSet.contentDescription = generateContentPreview(from: content)
        
        // Path and URL
        attributeSet.path = url.path
        attributeSet.contentURL = url
        
        // Content
        attributeSet.textContent = content
        
        // File metadata
        let fileExtension = url.pathExtension
        attributeSet.keywords = [fileName, fileExtension, "code", "editor"]
        
        // Determine content type based on extension
        if let utType = UTType(filenameExtension: fileExtension) {
            attributeSet.contentType = utType.identifier
        }
        
        // Add language/syntax info if available
        if let language = detectLanguage(from: fileExtension) {
            attributeSet.keywords?.append(language)
        }

struct SSHConnectionConfig: Codable, Identifiable, Equatable {
    var id: UUID = UUID()
    var name: String
    var host: String
    var port: Int
    var username: String
    var authMethod: SSHAuthMethod
    var lastUsed: Date?
    
    enum SSHAuthMethod: Codable, Equatable {
        case password(String)
        case privateKey(key: String, passphrase: String?)
    }
    
    static func == (lhs: SSHConnectionConfig, rhs: SSHConnectionConfig) -> Bool {
        lhs.id == rhs.id
    }
}

// MARK: - SSH Manager Delegate Protocol

protocol SSHManagerDelegate: AnyObject {
    func sshManagerDidConnect(_ manager: SSHManager)
    func sshManagerDidDisconnect(_ manager: SSHManager, error: Error?)
    func sshManager(_ manager: SSHManager, didReceiveOutput text: String)
    func sshManager(_ manager: SSHManager, didReceiveError text: String)
}

// MARK: - SSH Client Errors

enum SSHClientError: Error, LocalizedError {
    case connectionFailed(String)
    case authenticationFailed
    case channelCreationFailed
    case invalidChannelType
    case notConnected
    case timeout

import Foundation
import CommonCrypto

/// Cache entry containing a suggestion with its expiration timestamp
private final class CacheEntry {
    let suggestion: String
    let expirationDate: Date
    let contextHash: String
    
    init(suggestion: String, contextHash: String, ttl: TimeInterval) {
        self.suggestion = suggestion
        self.contextHash = contextHash
        self.expirationDate = Date().addingTimeInterval(ttl)
    }
    
    var isExpired: Bool {
        return Date() > expirationDate
    }
}

/// Thread-safe LRU cache for inline code suggestions with TTL expiration
/// Maximum 100 entries, 5 minute expiration
final class SuggestionCache {
    // MARK: - Constants
    
    static let maxEntries = 100
    static let expirationInterval: TimeInterval = 300 // 5 minutes
    
    // MARK: - Properties
    
    private var cache: [String: CacheEntry] = [:]
    /// Ordered list to track LRU access order (least recently used at the beginning)
    private var lruOrder: [String] = []
    
    /// Thread-safe queue for cache operations using concurrent queue with barriers
    private let cacheQueue = DispatchQueue(label: "com.vscodeipados.suggestioncache", attributes: .concurrent)
    
    // MARK: - Singleton
    
    static let shared = SuggestionCache()
    
    private init() {}
    
    // MARK: - Key Generation
    
    /// Generates a SHA256 hash from the code context string
    /// - Parameter context: The code context to hash
    /// - Returns: A hex-encoded SHA256 hash string
    private func hashContext(_ context: String) -> String {
        let data = Data(context.utf8)

import Foundation
import SwiftUI

// MARK: - Output Stream Types

/// Represents the source of an output line (stdout vs stderr)
enum OutputStreamType {
    case stdout
    case stderr
}

/// A single line entry in the output panel with metadata
struct TaskOutputLine: Identifiable, Equatable {
    let id: UUID
    let text: String
    let streamType: OutputStreamType
    let timestamp: Date
    var isAnsiFormatted: Bool = false
    var ansiAttributes: [NSRange: [NSAttributedString.Key: Any]]?
    
    init(
        id: UUID = UUID(),
        text: String,
        streamType: OutputStreamType = .stdout,
        timestamp: Date = Date(),
        isAnsiFormatted: Bool = false,
        ansiAttributes: [NSRange: [NSAttributedString.Key: Any]]? = nil
    ) {
        self.id = id
        self.text = text
        self.streamType = streamType
        self.timestamp = timestamp
        self.isAnsiFormatted = isAnsiFormatted
        self.ansiAttributes = ansiAttributes
    }
    
    static func == (lhs: TaskOutputLine, rhs: TaskOutputLine) -> Bool {
        lhs.id == rhs.id && lhs.text == rhs.text && lhs.streamType == rhs.streamType
    }
}

// MARK: - Output Channel

enum TaskOutputChannel: String, CaseIterable, Identifiable {
    case tasks = "Tasks"
    case git = "Git"
    case extensions = "Extensions"
    case remote = "Remote"  // New channel for remote execution

    var id: String { rawValue }
    
    var icon: String {
        switch self {
        case .tasks: return "checklist"
        case .git: return "arrow.branch"
        case .extensions: return "puzzlepiece"
        case .remote: return "network"
        }
    }

import SwiftUI
import UIKit

// MARK: - Window State

/// Represents the state of a single window that can be persisted and restored
struct WindowState: Codable, Identifiable {
    let id: UUID
    var workspacePath: String?
    var openFiles: [OpenFileState]
    var activeFileIndex: Int?
    var sidebarVisible: Bool
    var sidebarWidth: CGFloat
    var windowTitle: String?
    var lastModified: Date
    
    init(
        id: UUID = UUID(),
        workspacePath: String? = nil,
        openFiles: [OpenFileState] = [],
        activeFileIndex: Int? = nil,
        sidebarVisible: Bool = true,
        sidebarWidth: CGFloat = 250,
        windowTitle: String? = nil
    ) {
        self.id = id
        self.workspacePath = workspacePath
        self.openFiles = openFiles
        self.activeFileIndex = activeFileIndex
        self.sidebarVisible = sidebarVisible
        self.sidebarWidth = sidebarWidth
        self.windowTitle = windowTitle
        self.lastModified = Date()
    }
}

    var filePath: String

    var isUnsaved: Bool
    var unsavedContent: String?

        cursorColumn: Int = 1,

import Foundation
import UIKit

/// Highlights all occurrences of the currently selected word in the editor.
/// This is a key VSCode feature - when you select/double-click a word, all other
/// instances get a subtle background highlight.
final class WordOccurrenceHighlighter {
    static let shared = WordOccurrenceHighlighter()
    private init() {}
    
    // MARK: - Configuration
    
    /// Minimum word length to trigger highlighting
    var minimumWordLength: Int = 2
    
    /// Maximum occurrences to highlight (for performance)
    var maxOccurrences: Int = 500
    
    /// Whether highlighting is enabled
    var isEnabled: Bool = true
    
    // MARK: - Models
    
    struct Occurrence: Equatable {
        let range: NSRange
        let lineNumber: Int
    }
    
    // MARK: - Public API
    
    /// Finds all occurrences of the word at the given selection.
    /// Returns empty array if selection is not a valid word.
    func findOccurrences(in text: String, selection: NSRange) -> [Occurrence] {
        guard isEnabled else { return [] }
        guard !text.isEmpty else { return [] }
        
        // Get the selected word (or word at cursor)
        let selectedWord = getWordAtSelection(in: text, selection: selection)
        guard let word = selectedWord, word.count >= minimumWordLength else { return [] }
        
        // Don't highlight if selection spans multiple words
        if selection.length > 0 {
            let selectedText = (text as NSString).substring(with: selection)
            if selectedText.contains(" ") || selectedText.contains("\n") {
                return []
            }
        }
        
        return findAllOccurrences(of: word, in: text)
    }
    
    /// Finds all occurrences of a specific word in text.
    func findAllOccurrences(of word: String, in text: String) -> [Occurrence] {
        guard !word.isEmpty, !text.isEmpty else { return [] }
        
        var occurrences: [Occurrence] = []
        let nsText = text as NSString
        let textLength = nsText.length
        
        // Use word boundary matching for whole words only

import Foundation

// MARK: - Workspace Settings

/// Workspace (per-project) settings that override global settings.
///
/// These correspond loosely to VS Code's `.vscode/settings.json` keys:
/// - editor.tabSize
/// - editor.insertSpaces
/// - editor.formatOnSave
/// - workbench.colorTheme
/// - editor.fontFamily
/// - editor.fontSize
struct WorkspaceSettings: Codable, Equatable {
    /// Number of spaces that a tab represents.
    var tabSize: Int?

    /// Insert spaces when pressing tab.
    var insertSpaces: Bool?

    /// Format the document on save.
    var formatOnSave: Bool?

    /// Theme identifier (app-specific). Stored under `workbench.colorTheme`.
    var theme: String?

    /// Editor font family name.
    var fontFamily: String?

    /// Editor font size.
    var fontSize: Double?

    static let empty = WorkspaceSettings()
}

/// Concrete, fully-resolved settings after merging global + workspace overrides.
struct ResolvedWorkspaceSettings: Equatable {
    var tabSize: Int
    var insertSpaces: Bool
    var formatOnSave: Bool
    var theme: String
    var fontFamily: String
    var fontSize: Double
}

// MARK: - Workspace Manager

@MainActor
final class WorkspaceManager: ObservableObject {
    static let shared = WorkspaceManager()

    @Published private(set) var workspaceRootURL: URL?
    @Published private(set) var workspaceSettings: WorkspaceSettings = .empty

    @Published var lastErrorMessage: String?

    private init() {}

    // MARK: - Workspace

=== WorkspaceTrustManager.swift === (      39 lines)
import Foundation
import SwiftUI

@MainActor
final class WorkspaceTrustManager: ObservableObject {
    static let shared = WorkspaceTrustManager()
    
    @Published private(set) var trustedPaths: Set<String> = []
    
    private let trustedPathsKey = "workspace.trustedPaths"
    
    private init() {
        loadTrustedPaths()
    }
    
    func isTrusted(url: URL) -> Bool {
        return trustedPaths.contains(url.path)
    }
    
    func trust(url: URL) {
        trustedPaths.insert(url.path)
        saveTrustedPaths()
    }
    
    func revoke(url: URL) {
        trustedPaths.remove(url.path)
        saveTrustedPaths()
    }
    
    private func loadTrustedPaths() {
        if let saved = UserDefaults.standard.array(forKey: trustedPathsKey) as? [String] {
            trustedPaths = Set(saved)
        }
    }
    
    private func saveTrustedPaths() {
        UserDefaults.standard.set(Array(trustedPaths), forKey: trustedPathsKey)
    }
}

// SearchManager

// NOTE: This file is intentionally self-contained so it can integrate with varying app models.

        public var isWholeWord: Bool
        public var isRegex: Bool

                    isWholeWord: Bool = false,
                    isRegex: Bool = false) {
            self.isCaseSensitive = isCaseSensitive
            self.isWholeWord = isWholeWord
            self.isRegex = isRegex
        }
    }

        public var text: String
        public var options: SearchOptions
        /// Include file globs (e.g. ["**/*.swift", "**/*.md"]) - empty means include all.

        /// Exclude file globs (e.g. ["**/.git/**", "**/DerivedData/**"]).
        public var excludeGlobs: [String]

                    options: SearchOptions = .init(),
                    includeGlobs: [String] = [],
                    excludeGlobs: [String] = []) {
            self.text = text
            self.options = options
            self.includeGlobs = includeGlobs
            self.excludeGlobs = excludeGlobs
        }
    }

        public var column: Int
        public var length: Int

import Foundation
import SwiftUI
import UIKit

@MainActor
final class SettingsManager: ObservableObject {
    static let shared = SettingsManager()

    // MARK: - AppStorage keys

    @AppStorage("fontSize") var fontSize: Double = 14 {
        willSet { objectWillChange.send() }
    }

    @AppStorage("fontFamily") var fontFamily: String = "Menlo" {
        willSet { objectWillChange.send() }
    }

    @AppStorage("tabSize") var tabSize: Int = 4 {
        willSet { objectWillChange.send() }
    }

    @AppStorage("wordWrap") var wordWrap: Bool = true {
        willSet { objectWillChange.send() }
    }

    /// Mirrors SettingsView's picker tags: off / afterDelay / onFocusChange / onWindowChange
    @AppStorage("autoSave") var autoSaveRaw: String = AutoSaveMode.off.rawValue {
        willSet { objectWillChange.send() }
    }

    @AppStorage("minimapEnabled") var minimapEnabled: Bool = true {
        willSet { objectWillChange.send() }
    }

    private init() {}

    // MARK: - Types

    enum AutoSaveMode: String, CaseIterable {
        case off
        case afterDelay
        case onFocusChange
        case onWindowChange
    }

    var autoSaveMode: AutoSaveMode {
        get { AutoSaveMode(rawValue: autoSaveRaw) ?? .off }
        set { autoSaveRaw = newValue.rawValue }
    }

//  Note: SwiftNIO SSH doesn't have built-in SFTP, this provides the foundation
//

import Foundation
import NIO
import NIOSSH

// MARK: - SFTP Error Types

enum SFTPError: Error, LocalizedError {
    case notConnected
    case transferFailed(String)
    case fileNotFound
    case permissionDenied
    case directoryListFailed
    case operationCancelled
    
    var errorDescription: String? {
        switch self {
        case .notConnected: return "Not connected to server"
        case .transferFailed(let reason): return "Transfer failed: \(reason)"
        case .fileNotFound: return "File not found"
        case .permissionDenied: return "Permission denied"
        case .directoryListFailed: return "Failed to list directory"
        case .operationCancelled: return "Operation cancelled"
        }
    }
}

// MARK: - SFTP File Info

struct SFTPFileInfo: Identifiable {
    let id = UUID()
    let name: String
    let path: String
    let isDirectory: Bool
    let size: UInt64
    let modificationDate: Date?
    let permissions: String
}

    let fileName: String

import Foundation
import CoreSpotlight
import MobileCoreServices
import UniformTypeIdentifiers

/// Manages CoreSpotlight indexing for opened files
class SpotlightManager {
    static let shared = SpotlightManager()
    
    private let searchableIndex = CSSearchableIndex.default()
    private let domainIdentifier = "com.vscode.ipados.files"
    
    private init() {}
    
    // MARK: - Public API
    
    /// Index a file that was opened in the editor
    /// - Parameters:
    ///   - url: The file URL
    ///   - content: The file content
    ///   - fileName: The file name
    func indexFile(url: URL, content: String, fileName: String) {
        let attributeSet = CSSearchableItemAttributeSet(contentType: .content)
        
        // Basic attributes
        attributeSet.title = fileName
        attributeSet.displayName = fileName
        attributeSet.contentDescription = generateContentPreview(from: content)
        
        // Path and URL
        attributeSet.path = url.path
        attributeSet.contentURL = url
        
        // Content
        attributeSet.textContent = content
        
        // File metadata
        let fileExtension = url.pathExtension
        attributeSet.keywords = [fileName, fileExtension, "code", "editor"]
        
        // Determine content type based on extension
        if let utType = UTType(filenameExtension: fileExtension) {
            attributeSet.contentType = utType.identifier
        }
        
        // Add language/syntax info if available
        if let language = detectLanguage(from: fileExtension) {
            attributeSet.keywords?.append(language)
        }
        
---END PREVIEW---

//
//  NativeGitReader.swift
//  VSCodeiPadOS
//
//  Native Swift git repository reader - parses .git directory directly
//  Works offline without git binary (iOS compatible)
//

import Foundation
import Compression

// MARK: - Git Object Types

enum GitObjectType: String {
    case commit
    case tree
    case blob
    case tag
}

struct GitObject {
    let type: GitObjectType
    let size: Int
    let data: Data
}

// MARK: - Native Git Reader

class NativeGitReader {
    let repoURL: URL
    let gitDir: URL
    
    private var indexCache: GitIndex?
    private var headCache: String?
    
    init?(repositoryURL: URL) {
        self.repoURL = repositoryURL
        self.gitDir = repositoryURL.appendingPathComponent(".git")
        
        // Verify .git directory exists
        var isDir: ObjCBool = false
        guard FileManager.default.fileExists(atPath: gitDir.path, isDirectory: &isDir),
              isDir.boolValue else {
            return nil
        }
    }
    
    // MARK: - HEAD & Current Branch
    
    /// Read current branch name from .git/HEAD

=== NativeGitWriter.swift === (     615 lines)
//  NativeGitWriter.swift
//  VSCodeiPadOS
//
//  Minimal native git writer - writes loose objects and updates refs.
//  Supports local commit creation from staged (index) entries.
//

import Foundation
import Compression
import CommonCrypto

final class NativeGitWriter {
    let repoURL: URL
    let gitDir: URL

    init?(repositoryURL: URL) {
        self.repoURL = repositoryURL
        self.gitDir = repositoryURL.appendingPathComponent(".git")

        var isDir: ObjCBool = false
        guard FileManager.default.fileExists(atPath: gitDir.path, isDirectory: &isDir), isDir.boolValue else {
            return nil
        }
    }

    // MARK: - Public API

    /// Create a real local commit from the current index.
    /// - Returns: New commit SHA
    func commit(message: String, authorName: String = "VSCodeiPadOS", authorEmail: String = "vscode@localhost") throws -> String {
        // Build tree from stage-0 index entries
        let index = try readIndex()
        let entries = index.entries.filter { entry in
            // Git index stage is stored in flags bits 12-13
            let stage = (entry.flags >> 12) & 0x3
            return stage == 0
        }

        let root = TreeNode()
        for entry in entries {
            insert(entry: entry, into: root)
        }

        let treeSha = try writeTree(node: root)

        // Parent commit = current HEAD (if any)
        let parentSha = try headCommitSHA()

        // Create commit object
        let now = Date()
---END PREVIEW---
=== SSHGitClient.swift === (     451 lines)
//
//  SSHGitClient.swift
//  VSCodeiPadOS
//
//  SSH-based git client - runs git commands on remote servers
//  Used when native git reading isn't sufficient (push/pull/clone)
//

import Foundation
import NIO
import NIOSSH

// MARK: - SSH Git Client

class SSHGitClient {
    private let sshManager: SSHManager
    private var outputBuffer = ""
    private var errorBuffer = ""
    private var commandCompletion: ((Result<String, Error>) -> Void)?
    
    init(sshManager: SSHManager) {
        self.sshManager = sshManager
    }
    
    // MARK: - Git Commands
    
    /// Run git status and parse output
    func status(path: String) async throws -> SSHGitStatus {
        let output = try await runGitCommand(["status", "--porcelain=v2", "--branch"], in: path)
        return SSHGitStatus.parse(output: output)
    }
    
    /// Get current branch
    func currentBranch(path: String) async throws -> String {
        let output = try await runGitCommand(["branch", "--show-current"], in: path)
        let branch = output.trimmingCharacters(in: .whitespacesAndNewlines)
        return branch.isEmpty ? "HEAD" : branch
    }
    
    /// List all branches
    func branches(path: String) async throws -> [SSHGitBranch] {
        let output = try await runGitCommand(["branch", "-a", "--format=%(refname:short)|%(objectname:short)|%(upstream:short)|%(HEAD)"], in: path)
        
        var branches: [SSHGitBranch] = []
        for line in output.components(separatedBy: "\n") where !line.isEmpty {
            let parts = line.split(separator: "|", omittingEmptySubsequences: false).map(String.init)
            guard parts.count >= 4 else { continue }
            
            let name = parts[0]
            let sha = parts[1]
---END PREVIEW---

import Foundation
import Combine

// SearchManager
// Implements find-in-files style searching with options (case sensitivity, whole word, regex),
// include/exclude glob patterns, query history, and replace-in-files.
//
// NOTE: This file is intentionally self-contained so it can integrate with varying app models.

public final class SearchManager: ObservableObject {

    // MARK: - Types

    public struct SearchOptions: Codable, Hashable, Sendable {
        public var isCaseSensitive: Bool
        public var isWholeWord: Bool
        public var isRegex: Bool

        public init(isCaseSensitive: Bool = false,
                    isWholeWord: Bool = false,
                    isRegex: Bool = false) {
            self.isCaseSensitive = isCaseSensitive
            self.isWholeWord = isWholeWord
            self.isRegex = isRegex
        }
    }

    public struct SearchQuery: Codable, Hashable, Sendable {
        public var text: String
        public var options: SearchOptions
        /// Include file globs (e.g. ["**/*.swift", "**/*.md"]) - empty means include all.
        public var includeGlobs: [String]
        /// Exclude file globs (e.g. ["**/.git/**", "**/DerivedData/**"]).
        public var excludeGlobs: [String]

        public init(text: String,
                    options: SearchOptions = .init(),
                    includeGlobs: [String] = [],
                    excludeGlobs: [String] = []) {
            self.text = text
            self.options = options
            self.includeGlobs = includeGlobs
            self.excludeGlobs = excludeGlobs
        }
    }

    public struct MatchLocation: Codable, Hashable, Sendable {
        public var line: Int
        public var column: Int
        public var length: Int
---END PREVIEW---
=== SettingsManager.swift === (      98 lines)
import Foundation
import SwiftUI
import UIKit

@MainActor
final class SettingsManager: ObservableObject {
    static let shared = SettingsManager()

    // MARK: - AppStorage keys

    @AppStorage("fontSize") var fontSize: Double = 14 {
        willSet { objectWillChange.send() }
    }

    @AppStorage("fontFamily") var fontFamily: String = "Menlo" {
        willSet { objectWillChange.send() }
    }

    @AppStorage("tabSize") var tabSize: Int = 4 {
        willSet { objectWillChange.send() }
    }

    @AppStorage("wordWrap") var wordWrap: Bool = true {
        willSet { objectWillChange.send() }
    }

    /// Mirrors SettingsView's picker tags: off / afterDelay / onFocusChange / onWindowChange
    @AppStorage("autoSave") var autoSaveRaw: String = AutoSaveMode.off.rawValue {
        willSet { objectWillChange.send() }
    }

    @AppStorage("minimapEnabled") var minimapEnabled: Bool = true {
        willSet { objectWillChange.send() }
    }

    private init() {}

    // MARK: - Types

    enum AutoSaveMode: String, CaseIterable {
        case off
        case afterDelay
        case onFocusChange
        case onWindowChange
    }

    var autoSaveMode: AutoSaveMode {
        get { AutoSaveMode(rawValue: autoSaveRaw) ?? .off }
        set { autoSaveRaw = newValue.rawValue }
    }

//
//  SFTPManager.swift
//  VSCodeiPadOS
//
//  SFTP file transfer using SwiftNIO SSH
//  Note: SwiftNIO SSH doesn't have built-in SFTP, this provides the foundation
//

import Foundation
import NIO
import NIOSSH

// MARK: - SFTP Error Types

enum SFTPError: Error, LocalizedError {
    case notConnected
    case transferFailed(String)
    case fileNotFound
    case permissionDenied
    case directoryListFailed
    case operationCancelled
    
    var errorDescription: String? {
        switch self {
        case .notConnected: return "Not connected to server"
        case .transferFailed(let reason): return "Transfer failed: \(reason)"
        case .fileNotFound: return "File not found"
        case .permissionDenied: return "Permission denied"
        case .directoryListFailed: return "Failed to list directory"
        case .operationCancelled: return "Operation cancelled"
        }
    }
}

// MARK: - SFTP File Info

struct SFTPFileInfo: Identifiable {
    let id = UUID()
    let name: String
    let path: String
    let isDirectory: Bool
    let size: UInt64
    let modificationDate: Date?
    let permissions: String
}

// MARK: - Transfer Progress

struct SFTPTransferProgress {
    let fileName: String
---END PREVIEW---

import Foundation

// MARK: - Snippet Model

struct Snippet: Identifiable, Codable, Equatable {
    var id: UUID = UUID()

    /// Display name shown in pickers.
    var name: String

    /// The trigger text typed in the editor (expanded via prefix + Tab).
    var prefix: String

    /// The snippet template. Supports $1, $2 ... placeholders.
    var body: String

    /// Optional description shown in the UI.
    var description: String
}

// MARK: - Snippets Manager

final class SnippetsManager: ObservableObject {
    static let shared = SnippetsManager()

    @Published private(set) var customSnippets: [Snippet] = []

            prefix: "func",

=== SpotlightManager.swift === (     161 lines)
import Foundation
import CoreSpotlight
import MobileCoreServices
import UniformTypeIdentifiers

/// Manages CoreSpotlight indexing for opened files
class SpotlightManager {
    static let shared = SpotlightManager()
    
    private let searchableIndex = CSSearchableIndex.default()
    private let domainIdentifier = "com.vscode.ipados.files"
    
    private init() {}
    
    // MARK: - Public API
    
    /// Index a file that was opened in the editor
    /// - Parameters:
    ///   - url: The file URL
    ///   - content: The file content
    ///   - fileName: The file name
    func indexFile(url: URL, content: String, fileName: String) {
        let attributeSet = CSSearchableItemAttributeSet(contentType: .content)
        
        // Basic attributes
        attributeSet.title = fileName
        attributeSet.displayName = fileName
        attributeSet.contentDescription = generateContentPreview(from: content)
        
        // Path and URL
        attributeSet.path = url.path
        attributeSet.contentURL = url
        
        // Content
        attributeSet.textContent = content
        
        // File metadata
        let fileExtension = url.pathExtension
        attributeSet.keywords = [fileName, fileExtension, "code", "editor"]
        
        // Determine content type based on extension
        if let utType = UTType(filenameExtension: fileExtension) {
            attributeSet.contentType = utType.identifier
        }
        
        // Add language/syntax info if available
        if let language = detectLanguage(from: fileExtension) {
            attributeSet.keywords?.append(language)
        }
        
---END PREVIEW---

//
//  SSHManager.swift
//  VSCodeiPadOS
//
//  Stub SSH Manager - TODO: Implement with SwiftNIO SSH
//  Add package: https://github.com/apple/swift-nio-ssh
//

import Foundation
import SwiftUI

// MARK: - SSH Connection Model

struct SSHConnectionConfig: Codable, Identifiable, Equatable {
    var id: UUID = UUID()
    var name: String
    var host: String
    var port: Int
    var username: String
    var authMethod: SSHAuthMethod
    var lastUsed: Date?
    
    enum SSHAuthMethod: Codable, Equatable {
        case password(String)
        case privateKey(key: String, passphrase: String?)
    }
    
    static func == (lhs: SSHConnectionConfig, rhs: SSHConnectionConfig) -> Bool {
        lhs.id == rhs.id
    }
}

// MARK: - SSH Manager Delegate Protocol

protocol SSHManagerDelegate: AnyObject {
    func sshManagerDidConnect(_ manager: SSHManager)
    func sshManagerDidDisconnect(_ manager: SSHManager, error: Error?)
    func sshManager(_ manager: SSHManager, didReceiveOutput text: String)
    func sshManager(_ manager: SSHManager, didReceiveError text: String)
}

// MARK: - SSH Client Errors

enum SSHClientError: Error, LocalizedError {
    case connectionFailed(String)
    case authenticationFailed
    case channelCreationFailed
    case invalidChannelType
    case notConnected
    case timeout
---END PREVIEW---
=== SuggestionCache.swift === (     182 lines)
import Foundation
import CommonCrypto

/// Cache entry containing a suggestion with its expiration timestamp
private final class CacheEntry {
    let suggestion: String
    let expirationDate: Date
    let contextHash: String
    
    init(suggestion: String, contextHash: String, ttl: TimeInterval) {
        self.suggestion = suggestion
        self.contextHash = contextHash
        self.expirationDate = Date().addingTimeInterval(ttl)
    }
    
    var isExpired: Bool {
        return Date() > expirationDate
    }
}

/// Thread-safe LRU cache for inline code suggestions with TTL expiration
/// Maximum 100 entries, 5 minute expiration
final class SuggestionCache {
    // MARK: - Constants
    
    static let maxEntries = 100
    static let expirationInterval: TimeInterval = 300 // 5 minutes
    
    // MARK: - Properties
    
    private var cache: [String: CacheEntry] = [:]
    /// Ordered list to track LRU access order (least recently used at the beginning)
    private var lruOrder: [String] = []
    
    /// Thread-safe queue for cache operations using concurrent queue with barriers
    private let cacheQueue = DispatchQueue(label: "com.vscodeipados.suggestioncache", attributes: .concurrent)
    
    // MARK: - Singleton
    
    static let shared = SuggestionCache()
    
    private init() {}
    
    // MARK: - Key Generation
    
    /// Generates a SHA256 hash from the code context string
    /// - Parameter context: The code context to hash
    /// - Returns: A hex-encoded SHA256 hash string
    private func hashContext(_ context: String) -> String {
        let data = Data(context.utf8)
---END PREVIEW---

import Foundation
import Foundation
import SwiftUI

// MARK: - Execution Target

enum ExecutionTarget {
    case onDevice
    case remote
}

// MARK: - Supported Languages

/// Languages the app knows how to route to an execution backend.
///
/// - Note: On-device execution is currently supported for **JavaScript only**.
///         Other languages (python, swift, go, rust, etc.) are **remote-only**.
enum SupportedLanguage: String, CaseIterable, Sendable {
    // On-device supported
    case javascript = "javascript"

    // Remote-only (via SSH RemoteRunner)
    case swift = "swift"
    case python = "python"
    case python3 = "python3"
    case node = "node"
    case typescript = "typescript"
    case ruby = "ruby"
    case bash = "bash"
    case sh = "sh"

283|f96bbc|    // MARK: - Detection Helper Methods
284|b4687f|    
285|d80e09|    private func detectRegionStart(_ line: String) -> String? {
286|2a6674|        // C# / VS style regions
287|1989c4|        if line.hasPrefix("#region") {
288|08b9aa|            let components = line.components(separatedBy: "#region")
289|94d9b5|            return components.count > 1 ? components[1].trimmingCharacters(in: .whitespaces) : "Region"
290|5f3077|        }
291|39fca1|        // Objective-C pragma marks
292|667815|        if line.hasPrefix("#pragma mark") {
293|4621b4|            let components = line.components(separatedBy: "#pragma mark")
294|fec7ec|            return components.count > 1 ? components[1].trimmingCharacters(in: .whitespaces) : "Mark"
295|5f3077|        }
296|ccd53a|        // Swift MARK comments
297|b5e23f|        if line.contains("MARK:") {
298|504937|            let components = line.components(separatedBy: "MARK:")
299|fec7ec|            return components.count > 1 ? components[1].trimmingCharacters(in: .whitespaces) : "Mark"
300|5f3077|        }
301|c9afee|        // TODO/FIXME regions
302|453092|        if line.contains("TODO:") {
303|a4bc68|            let components = line.components(separatedBy: "TODO:")
304|5b14a7|            return "TODO: " + (components.count > 1 ? components[1].trimmingCharacters(in: .whitespaces) : "")
305|5f3077|        }
306|92ac0a|        if line.contains("FIXME:") {
307|0b0cff|            let components = line.components(separatedBy: "FIXME:")
308|218d1e|            return "FIXME: " + (components.count > 1 ? components[1].trimmingCharacters(in: .whitespaces) : "")
309|5f3077|        }
310|c2ae65|        return nil
311|27e597|    }
312|b4687f|    
313|c62450|    private func detectRegionEnd(_ line: String) -> Bool {
314|6c0837|        return line.hasPrefix("#endregion") || 
315|325a81|               line == "// MARK: -" ||
316|52ea28|               line.hasPrefix("#pragma mark -")
317|27e597|    }
318|b4687f|    
319|c6e97d|    private func detectCommentStart(_ line: String) -> Bool {
320|1cbff6|        return line.hasPrefix("/*") && !line.contains("*/")
321|27e597|    }
322|b4687f|    
323|ea9113|    private func detectCommentEnd(_ line: String) -> Bool {
324|d521b7|        return line.contains("*/") && !line.hasPrefix("/*")
325|27e597|    }

327|766920|    private func findConsecutiveImports(from startIndex: Int, in lines: [String]) -> Int {

331|25e1cc|            if trimmed.hasPrefix("import ") {
332|d549e2|                endIndex = i

353|763883|    private func detectDeclaration(_ line: String) -> (type: FoldRegion.FoldType, label: String)? {
354|3ccee6|        func matchName(pattern: String) -> String? {
355|6cc0c4|            guard let regex = try? NSRegularExpression(pattern: pattern, options: []) else { return nil }
356|f2f390|            guard let match = regex.firstMatch(in: line, range: NSRange(line.startIndex..., in: line)),

359|f5f042|            else { return nil }

362|adc83b|
363|f67078|        // Allow leading attributes like "@MainActor", "@available(...)"
364|0c5ed8|        // Allow common modifiers in any order (best-effort).
365|ca2c54|        let prefix = #"^(?:\s*@\w+(?:\([^\)]*\))?\s+)*(?:\s*(?:public|private|internal|fileprivate|open|final|indirect|lazy|static|override|mutating|nonmutating)\s+)*"#
366|adc83b|
367|cf49c1|        // IMPORTANT: avoid treating "class func"/"class var" as a type declaration.
368|e1fc33|        if let name = matchName(pattern: prefix + #"class\s+(?!func\b|var\b|let\b)([A-Za-z_]\w*)"#) {
369|d80de1|            return (.classOrStruct, name)
370|5f3077|        }
371|adc83b|

373|8d98f9|        if let name = matchName(pattern: prefix + #"actor\s+([A-Za-z_]\w*)"#) {

380|adc83b|

384|adc83b|

131|6224c3|    func updateSuggestions(for text: String, cursorPosition: Int) {

134|0efe98|            apply(items: [])

137|adc83b|
138|c2e8b0|        let prefixLower = context.prefix.lowercased()

140|bacf86|            // If user just typed a dot, show members even with empty prefix.
141|a46e04|            if context.isMemberCompletion, let base = context.memberBase {
142|c9f7f8|                let members = memberCandidates(forBase: base)

155|27a302|        candidates.append(contentsOf: symbols
156|488fb3|            .filter { $0.lowercased().hasPrefix(prefixLower) }

158|adc83b|

182|530c2a|    func selectNext() {

207|85ca8d|    func commitCurrentSuggestion(into text: inout String, cursorPosition: inout Int) {

210|2e795d|        guard let context = completionContext(in: text, cursorPosition: safeCursor) else { return }
211|adc83b|

224|7d7b95|    private struct CompletionContext {
225|0dee4e|        let prefix: String
226|b4787e|        let replacementRange: Range<String.Index>
227|56198f|        let isMemberCompletion: Bool
228|79772b|        let memberBase: String?
229|27e597|    }
230|adc83b|
231|f6601a|    private func completionContext(in text: String, cursorPosition: Int) -> CompletionContext? {
232|4c2df9|        guard !text.isEmpty else { return nil }
233|d6ed84|        
234|42915b|        // Safety check: ensure cursorPosition is within bounds
235|4b5e56|        let safeCursor = max(0, min(cursorPosition, text.count))
236|1fae78|        let cursorIndex = text.index(text.startIndex, offsetBy: safeCursor)
237|adc83b|
238|5aebf3|        // Find start of current identifier (letters/digits/_).
239|39f169|        var start = cursorIndex
240|26ebb3|        while start > text.startIndex {
241|c5c76e|            let prev = text.index(before: start)
242|9f8a31|            if isIdentifierChar(text[prev]) {
243|1929f8|                start = prev
244|f5a6eb|            } else {
245|74cfd8|                break
246|a7dc16|            }
247|5f3077|        }
248|adc83b|
249|bf292f|        let prefix = String(text[start..<cursorIndex])
250|adc83b|
251|824562|        // Member completion if immediately preceded by '.'
252|e374b4|        var isMember = false
253|e47c72|        var memberBase: String? = nil
254|adc83b|
255|0c2c38|        if start > text.startIndex {
256|2aa058|            let dotIndex = text.index(before: start)
257|88bd65|            if text[dotIndex] == "." {
258|ca8397|                isMember = true
259|adc83b|
260|6f6689|                // Parse identifier before '.'
261|91f615|                var baseEnd = dotIndex
262|521a36|                var baseStart = baseEnd
263|5fa357|                while baseStart > text.startIndex {
264|56e352|                    let prev = text.index(before: baseStart)
265|2a8ddd|                    if isIdentifierChar(text[prev]) {
266|fbabfa|                        baseStart = prev
267|540066|                    } else {
268|28a64b|                        break
269|c9717a|                    }
270|4e2d32|                }
271|f96abf|                let base = String(text[baseStart..<baseEnd])
272|ce472b|                if !base.isEmpty {
273|b9c992|                    memberBase = base
274|4e2d32|                }
275|a7dc16|            }
276|5f3077|        }
277|adc83b|
278|081616|        return CompletionContext(prefix: prefix,
279|c93ac4|                                 replacementRange: start..<cursorIndex,
280|a50c25|                                 isMemberCompletion: isMember,
281|ee9636|                                 memberBase: memberBase)
282|27e597|    }
283|adc83b|
284|818e4b|    private func isIdentifierChar(_ c: Character) -> Bool {
285|c233b9|        // Swift identifiers are more complex, but this is enough for basic autocomplete.
286|a164d4|        return c.isLetter || c.isNumber || c == "_"
287|27e597|    }
288|adc83b|
289|117bcf|    private func memberCandidates(forBase base: String) -> [String] {
290|cf1cb2|        // Basic heuristic: only match known stdlib types by exact name.
291|622249|        if let members = memberCompletions[base] { return members }
292|b72a10|        return []
293|27e597|    }
294|adc83b|
295|6ba6d8|    private func extractSymbols(from text: String) -> [String] {
296|1098cf|        // Very lightweight symbol extraction: looks for common declarations.
297|141450|        // Intentionally best-effort; keeps FEAT-046 self-contained.
298|f746d2|        var results = Set<String>()
299|adc83b|
300|f97d0d|        func addMatches(pattern: String) {
301|ca0afa|            guard let regex = try? NSRegularExpression(pattern: pattern, options: []) else { return }
302|6c811f|            let ns = text as NSString
303|b25f5b|            let range = NSRange(location: 0, length: ns.length)
304|d25847|            regex.enumerateMatches(in: text, options: [], range: range) { match, _, _ in
305|14f5aa|                guard let match, match.numberOfRanges >= 2 else { return }
306|345d9a|                let nameRange = match.range(at: 1)
307|de56e8|                guard nameRange.location != NSNotFound else { return }
308|8989a1|                let name = ns.substring(with: nameRange)
309|66b469|                if !name.isEmpty { results.insert(name) }
310|a7dc16|            }
311|5f3077|        }
312|adc83b|
313|a1c515|        // func Foo
314|cd4071|        addMatches(pattern: "\\bfunc\\s+([A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*)")
315|5eaefb|        // class/struct/enum/protocol/typealias Foo
316|685691|        addMatches(pattern: "\\b(?:class|struct|enum|protocol|typealias)\\s+([A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*)")
317|3764b8|        // let/var foo (captures first name before : = , )
318|bbf05c|        addMatches(pattern: "\\b(?:let|var)\\s+([A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*)(?=\\s*[:=,])")
319|adc83b|
320|01a90c|        return results.sorted { $0.localizedCaseInsensitiveCompare($1) == .orderedAscending }
321|27e597|    }
322|adc83b|
323|1470f2|    private func mergeAndSort(_ items: [Suggestion]) -> [Suggestion] {
324|ff1146|        // Keep highest-scored entry per id.
325|f6e288|        var bestById: [String: Suggestion] = [:]
326|686171|        for item in items {
327|0701eb|            if let existing = bestById[item.id] {
328|d5a6de|                if item.score > existing.score {
329|763640|                    bestById[item.id] = item
330|4e2d32|                }
331|f5a6eb|            } else {
332|b949a2|                bestById[item.id] = item
333|a7dc16|            }
334|5f3077|        }
335|adc83b|
336|cfe5f9|        return bestById.values.sorted {
337|708c2d|            if $0.score != $1.score { return $0.score > $1.score }
338|e5aa71|            return $0.displayText.localizedCaseInsensitiveCompare($1.displayText) == .orderedAscending
339|5f3077|        }
340|27e597|    }
341|adc83b|
342|ba218c|    private func apply(items: [Suggestion]) {
343|02d13b|        suggestionItems = items
344|f6da05|        suggestions = items.map { $0.displayText }
345|c77b90|        showSuggestions = !items.isEmpty
346|81a6a8|        selectedIndex = 0
347|27e597|    }
348|b31277|}
349|adc83b|
350|c6407f|// MARK: - Safe Array Access
351|adc83b|
352|d72239|private extension Array {
353|686e51|    subscript(safe index: Int) -> Element? {
354|0b415a|        indices.contains(index) ? self[index] : nil
355|27e597|    }

233|11024e|        var stringChar: Character = "\""
234|d6ed84|        
235|f6da67|        // For backward search, we need a simpler approach since escape sequences are tricky
236|fe2046|        // We'll do a basic scan that handles most common cases
237|267cd6|        for i in stride(from: startIndex - 1, through: 0, by: -1) {
238|8672ad|            let char = chars[i]
239|3070d1|            
240|1d0b12|            // Simple string detection (not perfect but handles common cases)
241|631cb5|            if char == "\"" || char == "'" {
242|9df61c|                // Check if escaped
243|d575e8|                if i > 0 && chars[i - 1] == "\\" {
244|f41bef|                    continue
245|4e2d32|                }
246|399b31|                inString = !inString
247|54e155|                stringChar = char
248|1df510|                continue
249|a7dc16|            }
250|3070d1|            
251|28e059|            if inString { continue }
252|3070d1|            
253|b67e2d|            if char == type.close {
254|4994cd|                depth += 1
255|d1d7f1|            } else if char == type.open {
256|ae2b9f|                depth -= 1
257|f13da5|                if depth == 0 {
258|125c3c|                    return i
259|4e2d32|                }
260|a7dc16|            }
261|5f3077|        }
262|d6ed84|        
263|c2ae65|        return nil
264|27e597|    }

273|4a758d|            UIColor(red: 0.94, green: 0.78, blue: 0.18, alpha: 1.0),  // Gold
274|9757eb|            UIColor(red: 0.85, green: 0.44, blue: 0.84, alpha: 1.0),  // Purple
275|42106c|            UIColor(red: 0.36, green: 0.72, blue: 0.82, alpha: 1.0),  // Cyan

277|d0845d|            UIColor(red: 0.98, green: 0.55, blue: 0.38, alpha: 1.0),  // Orange
278|922ac4|            UIColor(red: 0.64, green: 0.51, blue: 0.91, alpha: 1.0),  // Lavender
279|220505|        ]
280|b30a12|        return colors[depth % colors.count]
281|27e597|    }

28|97d12a|    func executeCurrentFile(fileName: String, content: String) {
29|cc9311|        let fileExtension = (fileName as NSString).pathExtension.lowercased()
30|d6ed84|        
31|2eea91|        switch fileExtension {
32|ff039a|        case "js", "mjs":
33|760a4e|            executeJavaScript(fileName: fileName, code: content)
34|048ca0|        case "ts", "tsx":
35|b896b9|            outputManager.append("[ERROR] TypeScript execution requires transpilation. Coming soon!", to: OutputChannel.javascript, streamType: StreamType.stderr)
36|de8aa1|            outputManager.selectedChannel = OutputChannel.javascript

39|25499b|            outputManager.selectedChannel = OutputChannel.python

41|cc7290|            outputManager.append("[ERROR] No runner available for .\(fileExtension) files", to: OutputChannel.output, streamType: StreamType.stderr)
42|c75b79|            outputManager.selectedChannel = OutputChannel.output

48|d76b7d|    private func executeJavaScript(fileName: String, code: String) {
49|0dec37|        // Switch to JavaScript output channel
50|e39e9c|        outputManager.selectedChannel = OutputChannel.javascript
51|d6ed84|        
52|a728d5|        // Clear previous output and show execution header
53|1511a8|        outputManager.clear(OutputChannel.javascript)
54|6cfb10|        outputManager.append("▶ Running: \(fileName)", to: OutputChannel.javascript)
55|89174a|        outputManager.append("─────────────────────────────────────", to: OutputChannel.javascript)
56|d6ed84|        
57|a3f62b|        // Create a fresh JSRunner instance
58|462f68|        jsRunner = JSRunner()
59|d6ed84|        
60|e61664|        // Set up console handler to capture output
61|305ad3|        jsRunner?.setConsoleHandler { [weak self] message in
62|270dfc|            Task { @MainActor in
63|150252|                guard let self = self else { return }
64|216278|                
65|b169c6|                // Determine stream type based on message prefix
66|27b18b|                let streamType: StreamType = message.hasPrefix("[ERROR]") || message.hasPrefix("[EXCEPTION]") ? StreamType.stderr : StreamType.stdout
67|216278|                
68|4b39b7|                // Remove the prefix for cleaner output display
69|62636a|                var cleanMessage = message
70|121995|                for prefix in ["[LOG] ", "[INFO] ", "[WARN] ", "[ERROR] ", "[EXCEPTION] "] {
71|1df682|                    if message.hasPrefix(prefix) {
72|11af1f|                        cleanMessage = String(message.dropFirst(prefix.count))
73|28a64b|                        break
74|c9717a|                    }
75|4e2d32|                }
76|216278|                
77|6a3616|                self.outputManager.append(cleanMessage, to: OutputChannel.javascript, streamType: streamType)
78|a7dc16|            }
79|5f3077|        }
80|d6ed84|        
81|5a9195|        // Execute the code asynchronously
82|0f1953|        Task {
83|073be0|            let startTime = Date()
84|3070d1|            
85|71cc09|            do {
86|f3a1fd|                let result = try await jsRunner?.execute(code: code)
87|1ee120|                let duration = Date().timeIntervalSince(startTime)
88|216278|

90|f23508|                if let result = result, !result.isUndefined {
91|8a7075|                    let resultString = formatJSValue(result)
92|174733|                    outputManager.append("─────────────────────────────────────", to: OutputChannel.javascript)
93|1c40a4|                    outputManager.append("⮐ Result: \(resultString)", to: OutputChannel.javascript)
94|4e2d32|                }
95|216278|                
96|b2c6e7|                // Show completion message
97|dc27a4|                outputManager.append("─────────────────────────────────────", to: OutputChannel.javascript)
98|2c8c65|                outputManager.append("✓ Completed in \(String(format: "%.2f", duration * 1000))ms", to: OutputChannel.javascript)
99|216278|                
100|77a0dc|            } catch let error as JSRunnerError {
101|dc27a4|                outputManager.append("─────────────────────────────────────", to: OutputChannel.javascript)
102|a0d863|                outputManager.append("✗ Error: \(error.localizedDescription)", to: OutputChannel.javascript, streamType: StreamType.stderr)
103|216278|                
104|91b9a2|            } catch {
105|dc27a4|                outputManager.append("─────────────────────────────────────", to: OutputChannel.javascript)
106|a0d863|                outputManager.append("✗ Error: \(error.localizedDescription)", to: OutputChannel.javascript, streamType: StreamType.stderr)
107|a7dc16|            }
108|3070d1|            
109|eb797f|            // Clean up runner
110|e9e019|            jsRunner = nil
111|5f3077|        }

116|7aa5bd|    /// Format a JSValue for display
117|e68bfd|    private func formatJSValue(_ value: JSValue) -> String {
118|c903ed|        if value.isNull {
119|c04d4d|            return "null"
120|35d02e|        } else if value.isUndefined {
121|b32932|            return "undefined"
122|edfa6b|        } else if value.isBoolean {
123|1a76ee|            return value.toBool() ? "true" : "false"
124|4e414f|        } else if value.isNumber {
125|9abc83|            let num = value.toNumber()
126|d3fc6f|            // Check if it's an integer
127|a09f20|            if let n = num, n.doubleValue == Double(n.intValue) {
128|ee9ad8|                return "\(n.intValue)"
129|a7dc16|            }
130|64a140|            return "\(num?.doubleValue ?? 0)"
131|419991|        } else if value.isString {
132|7c8a45|            return "\"\(value.toString() ?? "")\""
133|a72377|        } else if value.isArray {
134|d7f252|            if let array = value.toArray() {
135|c35363|                return "[\(array.map { "\($0)" }.joined(separator: ", "))]"
136|a7dc16|            }
137|976c47|            return "[]"
138|bb3c98|        } else if value.isObject {
139|b4fae0|            if let dict = value.toDictionary() {
140|ee1ab7|                let json = try? JSONSerialization.data(withJSONObject: dict, options: [.prettyPrinted, .sortedKeys])
141|a9b0f4|                if let json = json, let str = String(data: json, encoding: .utf8) {
142|cfed56|                    return str
143|4e2d32|                }
144|a7dc16|            }
145|0b436d|            return value.toString() ?? "[object]"
146|5f3077|        }
147|bbc620|        return value.toString() ?? "undefined"
148|27e597|    }
149|b31277|}

72|fb8c13|            candidates = ["CourierNewPSMT", "Courier New"]

75|0298e7|        case "Fira Code":
76|dc7760|            candidates = ["FiraCode-Regular", "Fira Code"]
77|30da51|        case "JetBrains Mono":

79|520b7d|        default:
80|d7ec8a|            candidates = [family]
81|5f3077|        }
82|adc83b|
83|ef3c25|        for name in candidates {
84|b0cf4a|            if let font = UIFont(name: name, size: size) {
85|404673|                // Ensure monospaced feel if available; otherwise return as-is.
86|6dcd7e|                return font
87|a7dc16|            }
88|5f3077|        }
89|adc83b|
90|c377bd|        // Fallback
91|0966c5|        return UIFont.monospacedSystemFont(ofSize: size, weight: .regular)
92|27e597|    }

3|6505f2|/// A lightweight inlay-hints engine used to show inline type annotations.
4|63c993|///
5|18e543|/// Scope (FEAT-133):
6|1f06c1|/// - Detect simple Swift variable declarations without explicit types.
7|6b0114|/// - Infer basic types from the initializer expression.
8|b1b02e|/// - Return hint objects with line/column + a display string (e.g. ": Int").
9|4f8cca|final class InlayHintsManager {
10|5960e0|    static let shared = InlayHintsManager()
11|54113e|    private init() {}
12|adc83b|
13|d9f5b5|    // MARK: - Models
14|adc83b|
15|14e2da|    struct InlayHint: Identifiable, Equatable {
16|d16339|        let id = UUID()
17|adc83b|
18|7a2975|        /// 0-based line index.
19|c0eddc|        let line: Int
20|adc83b|
21|efa22a|        /// 0-based UTF16 column index within the line.
22|1b9532|        let column: Int
23|adc83b|
24|c52f44|        /// Render-ready hint text (e.g. ": Int").
25|aa57fc|        let text: String
26|27e597|    }
27|adc83b|
28|36e755|    // MARK: - Public API
29|adc83b|
30|2ce197|    func hints(for code: String, language: CodeLanguage) -> [InlayHint] {
31|b95802|        guard language == .swift else { return [] }
32|bb2b0b|        return swiftTypeHints(for: code)
33|27e597|    }
34|adc83b|
35|52c601|    // MARK: - Swift parsing
36|adc83b|
37|16c9d6|    private func swiftTypeHints(for code: String) -> [InlayHint] {
38|fdeff0|        let lines = code.components(separatedBy: .newlines)
39|46c824|        var result: [InlayHint] = []
40|adc83b|
41|225f78|        // Matches:
42|e2c046|        //   let x = 5
43|2cac4f|        //   var name = "abc"
44|21217b|        // Avoids explicit type annotations because the pattern does not accept `:` before `=`.
45|49e410|        let pattern = "^\\s*(?:let|var)\\s+([A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*)\\s*=\\s*(.+)$"
46|72432c|        let regex = try? NSRegularExpression(pattern: pattern, options: [])
47|adc83b|
48|d1a1ab|        for (lineIndex, line) in lines.enumerated() {
49|cbe876|            let trimmed = line.trimmingCharacters(in: .whitespacesAndNewlines)
50|9b2e7c|            guard !trimmed.hasPrefix("//"), !trimmed.isEmpty else { continue }
51|adc83b|
52|18a8aa|            let nsLine = line as NSString
53|89c109|            let range = NSRange(location: 0, length: nsLine.length)
54|c471d9|            guard let match = regex?.firstMatch(in: line, options: [], range: range) else { continue }
55|ad3867|            guard match.numberOfRanges >= 3 else { continue }
56|adc83b|
57|423174|            let nameRange = match.range(at: 1)
58|3c5579|            let rhsRange = match.range(at: 2)
59|7a7150|            if nameRange.location == NSNotFound || rhsRange.location == NSNotFound { continue }
60|adc83b|
61|165da4|            // Basic sanity checks: ignore tuple bindings like `let (a, b) = ...`
62|679645|            let name = nsLine.substring(with: nameRange)
63|5c9927|            guard !name.isEmpty else { continue }
64|adc83b|
65|666b05|            // RHS, stripped of trailing `//` comments (naive but practical).
66|53413c|            var rhs = nsLine.substring(with: rhsRange)
67|8beea4|            if let commentRange = rhs.range(of: "//") {
68|a56309|                rhs = String(rhs[..<commentRange.lowerBound])
69|a7dc16|            }
70|107569|            rhs = rhs.trimmingCharacters(in: .whitespacesAndNewlines)
71|adc83b|
72|8f6e07|            // Infer a type. If we can’t infer, don’t create a hint.
73|992808|            guard let inferred = inferSwiftType(from: rhs) else { continue }
74|adc83b|
75|ed8c63|            // Column for hint placement = end of identifier.
76|f4b52b|            let hintColumn = nameRange.location + nameRange.length
77|32af9e|            result.append(InlayHint(line: lineIndex, column: hintColumn, text: ": \(inferred)"))
78|5f3077|        }
79|adc83b|
80|67ed82|        return result
81|27e597|    }
82|adc83b|
83|014518|    // MARK: - Swift type inference (basic)
84|adc83b|
85|e4ea7a|    private func inferSwiftType(from rawExpression: String) -> String? {
86|3784b4|        let expr = rawExpression.trimmingCharacters(in: .whitespacesAndNewlines)
87|a14679|        guard !expr.isEmpty else { return nil }
88|adc83b|
89|ea35f3|        // Bool
90|62989c|        if expr == "true" || expr == "false" { return "Bool" }
91|adc83b|
92|5f3b7c|        // String literal (simple)
93|553d7e|        if expr.hasPrefix("\"") { return "String" }
94|adc83b|
95|f70754|        // Numeric: Int
96|b5aa27|        if matches(expr, pattern: "^-?\\d+$") { return "Int" }
97|f87d78|        if matches(expr, pattern: "^-?0x[0-9A-Fa-f]+$") { return "Int" }
98|212646|        if matches(expr, pattern: "^-?0b[01]+$") { return "Int" }
99|d18d43|        if matches(expr, pattern: "^-?0o[0-7]+$") { return "Int" }
100|adc83b|
101|e6218e|        // Numeric: Double (Swift defaults floating-point literals to Double)
102|53b861|        if matches(expr, pattern: "^-?\\d+\\.\\d+(?:[eE][-+]?\\d+)?$") { return "Double" }
103|71e012|        if matches(expr, pattern: "^-?\\d+(?:[eE][-+]?\\d+)$") { return "Double" }
104|adc83b|
105|69d592|        // Array / Dictionary literals
106|20ec44|        if expr.hasPrefix("[") {
107|ce2ebb|            if let dictType = inferDictionaryType(from: expr) { return dictType }
108|f27fb4|            if let arrayType = inferArrayType(from: expr) { return arrayType }
109|5f3077|        }
110|adc83b|
111|c1c62e|        // Constructor-looking calls: `TypeName(...)` (biased toward UpperCamelCase types)
112|c8a287|        if let typeName = firstRegexGroup(expr, pattern: "^([A-Z][A-Za-z0-9_]*)\\s*\\(") {
113|7cdb04|            return typeName
114|5f3077|        }
115|adc83b|
116|9ff15e|        // Enum/static member: `TypeName.member`
117|7c8b37|        if let typeName = firstRegexGroup(expr, pattern: "^([A-Z][A-Za-z0-9_]*)\\.[A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*$") {
118|7cdb04|            return typeName
119|5f3077|        }
120|adc83b|
121|c2ae65|        return nil
122|27e597|    }
123|adc83b|
124|ebc532|    private func inferArrayType(from expr: String) -> String? {
125|53c5a0|        let trimmed = expr.trimmingCharacters(in: .whitespacesAndNewlines)
126|c8c4b9|        guard trimmed.hasPrefix("[") && trimmed.hasSuffix("]") else { return nil }
127|adc83b|
128|81b2b5|        let inner = trimmed.dropFirst().dropLast().trimmingCharacters(in: .whitespacesAndNewlines)
129|4d16d8|        if inner.isEmpty { return "[Any]" }
130|adc83b|
131|bdbc65|        // Take the first top-level element (naive: split by comma).
132|51ddab|        let firstPart = inner.split(separator: ",", maxSplits: 1, omittingEmptySubsequences: true).first
133|3197f4|        guard let first = firstPart.map(String.init)?.trimmingCharacters(in: .whitespacesAndNewlines), !first.isEmpty else {
134|88e905|            return "[Any]"
135|5f3077|        }
136|adc83b|
137|e844da|        if let elementType = inferSwiftType(from: first) {
138|655e59|            return "[\(elementType)]"
139|5f3077|        }
140|adc83b|
141|77d208|        return "[Any]"
142|27e597|    }
143|adc83b|
144|f7b9b1|    private func inferDictionaryType(from expr: String) -> String? {
145|53c5a0|        let trimmed = expr.trimmingCharacters(in: .whitespacesAndNewlines)
146|c8c4b9|        guard trimmed.hasPrefix("[") && trimmed.hasSuffix("]") else { return nil }
147|adc83b|
148|85eff8|        // Fast check: dictionary literals must contain `:` somewhere.
149|8a247d|        guard trimmed.contains(":") else { return nil }
150|adc83b|
151|81b2b5|        let inner = trimmed.dropFirst().dropLast().trimmingCharacters(in: .whitespacesAndNewlines)
152|be74cb|        if inner.isEmpty { return "[AnyHashable: Any]" }
153|adc83b|
154|141281|        // Grab the first `key: value` pair (naive: up to first comma).
155|53580e|        let firstPair = inner.split(separator: ",", maxSplits: 1, omittingEmptySubsequences: true).first.map(String.init) ?? ""
156|2ae14f|        guard let colonIndex = firstPair.firstIndex(of: ":") else { return nil }
157|adc83b|
158|90fe28|        let keyExpr = String(firstPair[..<colonIndex]).trimmingCharacters(in: .whitespacesAndNewlines)
159|c8c196|        let valueExpr = String(firstPair[firstPair.index(after: colonIndex)...]).trimmingCharacters(in: .whitespacesAndNewlines)
160|adc83b|
161|28ea5b|        let keyType = inferSwiftType(from: keyExpr) ?? "AnyHashable"
162|eb3fdd|        let valueType = inferSwiftType(from: valueExpr) ?? "Any"
163|31573c|        return "[\(keyType): \(valueType)]"
164|27e597|    }
165|adc83b|
166|e21b8f|    // MARK: - Regex helpers
167|adc83b|
168|3efaff|    private func matches(_ text: String, pattern: String) -> Bool {
169|695372|        (try? NSRegularExpression(pattern: pattern, options: []))?.firstMatch(
170|7697e1|            in: text,
171|11ba8c|            options: [],
172|264c2c|            range: NSRange(location: 0, length: (text as NSString).length)
173|d36f78|        ) != nil
174|27e597|    }
175|adc83b|
176|133a2e|    private func firstRegexGroup(_ text: String, pattern: String) -> String? {
177|473ca5|        guard let regex = try? NSRegularExpression(pattern: pattern, options: []) else { return nil }
178|c60005|        let ns = text as NSString
179|05fef3|        let range = NSRange(location: 0, length: ns.length)
180|090ffd|        guard let match = regex.firstMatch(in: text, options: [], range: range), match.numberOfRanges >= 2 else { return nil }
181|5ad252|        let r = match.range(at: 1)
182|8bd644|        guard r.location != NSNotFound else { return nil }
183|2550a5|        return ns.substring(with: r)
184|27e597|    }
185|b31277|}

21|fe2801|/// Thread-safe LRU cache for inline code suggestions with TTL expiration
22|e5ec5c|/// Maximum 100 entries, 5 minute expiration
23|4db88e|final class SuggestionCache {
24|f620b3|    // MARK: - Constants
25|b4687f|    
26|21ee5c|    static let maxEntries = 100
27|d2414b|    static let expirationInterval: TimeInterval = 300 // 5 minutes
28|b4687f|    
29|6503ae|    // MARK: - Properties
30|b4687f|    
31|34e47f|    private var cache: [String: CacheEntry] = [:]
32|48d9ba|    /// Ordered list to track LRU access order (least recently used at the beginning)
33|830760|    private var lruOrder: [String] = []
34|b4687f|    
35|dd918f|    /// Thread-safe queue for cache operations using concurrent queue with barriers
36|a3a1ae|    private let cacheQueue = DispatchQueue(label: "com.vscodeipados.suggestioncache", attributes: .concurrent)
37|b4687f|    
38|db9bbb|    // MARK: - Singleton
39|b4687f|    
40|8f377e|    static let shared = SuggestionCache()
41|b4687f|    
42|54113e|    private init() {}
43|b4687f|    
44|fb9d4d|    // MARK: - Key Generation
45|b4687f|    
46|938b88|    /// Generates a SHA256 hash from the code context string
47|62811f|    /// - Parameter context: The code context to hash
48|d02457|    /// - Returns: A hex-encoded SHA256 hash string
49|233ae6|    private func hashContext(_ context: String) -> String {
50|ab8594|        let data = Data(context.utf8)
51|97a5e1|        var hash = [UInt8](repeating: 0, count: Int(CC_SHA256_DIGEST_LENGTH))
52|3e64b0|        _ = data.withUnsafeBytes { bytes in
53|3e6f69|            CC_SHA256(bytes.baseAddress, CC_LONG(data.count), &hash)
54|5f3077|        }
55|f6441a|        return hash.map { String(format: "%02hhx", $0) }.joined()
56|27e597|    }
57|b4687f|    
58|36e755|    // MARK: - Public API
59|b4687f|    
60|033bf4|    /// Retrieves a cached suggestion for the given code context if it exists and hasn't expired.
61|c435d3|    /// Updates LRU order on successful retrieval.
62|112b9c|    /// - Parameter context: The code context string to look up
63|6d9dea|    /// - Returns: The cached suggestion string, or nil if not found or expired
64|5ba297|    func get(context: String) -> String? {
65|a9c444|        let key = hashContext(context)
66|d6ed84|        
67|98f033|        return cacheQueue.sync {
68|9d266a|            guard let entry = cache[key] else {
69|15034a|                return nil
70|a7dc16|            }
71|3070d1|            
72|2f14e2|            // Check if entry has expired
73|e16ac4|            if entry.isExpired {
74|89aad4|                // Remove expired entry
75|185e7a|                cache.removeValue(forKey: key)
76|8d693c|                removeFromLRU(key)
77|15034a|                return nil
78|a7dc16|            }
79|3070d1|            
80|bff744|            // Update LRU order - move to end (most recently used)
81|960881|            updateLRUOrder(key)
82|3070d1|            
83|c5897e|            return entry.suggestion
84|5f3077|        }
85|27e597|    }
86|b4687f|    
87|c07d11|    /// Stores a suggestion for the given code context in the cache.
88|94f7ba|    /// Evicts oldest entry if cache is at max capacity.
89|45b4bd|    /// - Parameters:
90|3b7936|    ///   - context: The code context string (used as cache key via SHA256 hash)
91|0e56d7|    ///   - suggestion: The AI suggestion to cache
92|682096|    func set(context: String, suggestion: String) {
93|a9c444|        let key = hashContext(context)
94|d6ed84|        
95|2eb06b|        cacheQueue.async(flags: .barrier) { [weak self] in
96|2baf81|            guard let self = self else { return }
97|3070d1|            
98|48bad1|            // Create new entry with TTL
99|464f63|            let entry = CacheEntry(
100|47deb4|                suggestion: suggestion,
101|95552d|                contextHash: key,
102|3a2d94|                ttl: Self.expirationInterval
103|43d041|            )
104|3070d1|            
105|9c2fb2|            // Check if this is an update to existing entry
106|1b247c|            if self.cache[key] != nil {
107|b09099|                // Update existing entry
108|04cd95|                self.cache[key] = entry
109|664568|                self.updateLRUOrder(key)
110|f5a6eb|            } else {
111|a4567b|                // Check if we need to evict oldest entry
112|20b4bf|                if self.cache.count >= Self.maxEntries {
113|f4766d|                    self.evictLRUEntry()
114|4e2d32|                }
115|216278|                
116|e926f1|                // Add new entry
117|04cd95|                self.cache[key] = entry
118|d4d7a7|                self.lruOrder.append(key)
119|a7dc16|            }
120|5f3077|        }
121|27e597|    }
122|b4687f|    
123|06e36f|    /// Clears all entries from the cache.
124|5416f6|    func clear() {
125|2eb06b|        cacheQueue.async(flags: .barrier) { [weak self] in
126|1e7e79|            self?.cache.removeAll()
127|a434d2|            self?.lruOrder.removeAll()
128|5f3077|        }
129|27e597|    }
130|b4687f|    
131|81a958|    // MARK: - Private Helpers
132|b4687f|    
133|e268ba|    /// Removes a key from the LRU order tracking
134|9850b8|    private func removeFromLRU(_ key: String) {
135|acf9f5|        lruOrder.removeAll { $0 == key }
136|27e597|    }
137|b4687f|    
138|4e5908|    /// Updates LRU order by moving the accessed key to the end (most recently used)
139|e5b0f9|    private func updateLRUOrder(_ key: String) {
140|33983e|        removeFromLRU(key)
141|b3c36a|        lruOrder.append(key)
142|27e597|    }
143|b4687f|    
144|1d8cd3|    /// Evicts the least recently used entry from the cache
145|9bba21|    private func evictLRUEntry() {
146|1374a3|        guard !lruOrder.isEmpty else { return }
147|d6ed84|        
148|4b277f|        let oldestKey = lruOrder.removeFirst()
149|5cda32|        cache.removeValue(forKey: oldestKey)
150|27e597|    }
151|b4687f|    
152|70f772|    // MARK: - Debug/Testing
153|b4687f|    
154|897b17|    /// Returns the current number of cached entries (for testing/debugging purposes)
155|26c304|    var count: Int {
156|98f033|        return cacheQueue.sync {
157|14ea56|            // Clean up expired entries before counting
158|a1af1c|            let now = Date()
159|bfa8e1|            let expiredKeys = cache.filter { $0.value.expirationDate < now }.map { $0.key }
160|b44dd2|            for key in expiredKeys {
161|185e7a|                cache.removeValue(forKey: key)
162|8d693c|                removeFromLRU(key)
163|a7dc16|            }
164|27c5f5|            return cache.count
165|5f3077|        }
166|27e597|    }
167|b4687f|    
168|55c124|    /// Removes all expired entries from the cache
169|0ac3ae|    func cleanupExpired() {
170|2eb06b|        cacheQueue.async(flags: .barrier) { [weak self] in
171|2baf81|            guard let self = self else { return }
172|3070d1|            
173|a1af1c|            let now = Date()
174|9b20de|            let expiredKeys = self.cache.filter { $0.value.expirationDate < now }.map { $0.key }
175|3070d1|            
176|b44dd2|            for key in expiredKeys {
177|a42745|                self.cache.removeValue(forKey: key)
178|98338e|                self.removeFromLRU(key)
179|a7dc16|            }
180|5f3077|        }
181|27e597|    }

158|cb81b4|    private func canonicalFileId(_ file: String) -> String {
159|e998da|        // Many call sites use URL.absoluteString; convert file:// URLs to paths so we
160|b513c2|        // don't end up with duplicate breakpoint buckets for the same file.
161|9942cc|        if let url = URL(string: file), url.isFileURL {
162|bdef50|            return url.path
163|5f3077|        }

167|de10cc|    /// Normalizes UI-provided line indices.
168|2cbc84|    ///
169|20e2d8|    /// The editor UI typically uses 0-based indices for line iteration. We clamp
170|6014a1|    /// to a minimum of 0 to avoid negative values.
171|b22942|    private func canonicalLine(_ line: Int) -> Int {
172|20f91b|        max(0, line)
173|27e597|    }
174|adc83b|
175|5ce9fa|    func hasBreakpoint(file: String, line: Int) -> Bool {
176|345f2b|        let fileId = canonicalFileId(file)
177|e74a32|        let line = canonicalLine(line)
178|c135e6|        return breakpointsByFile[fileId]?.contains(line) == true
179|27e597|    }
180|adc83b|
181|c99ea4|    func toggleBreakpoint(file: String, line: Int) {
182|345f2b|        let fileId = canonicalFileId(file)
183|e74a32|        let line = canonicalLine(line)
184|adc83b|

240|144775|    @discardableResult
241|923c00|    func addWatchExpression(_ expression: String, initialValue: String = "—") -> WatchExpression? {
242|074c5b|        let trimmed = expression.trimmingCharacters(in: .whitespacesAndNewlines)
243|e44359|        guard !trimmed.isEmpty else { return nil }
244|adc83b|
245|8392ab|        // Avoid duplicates by expression text.
246|08eef3|        if watchExpressions.contains(where: { $0.expression == trimmed }) {
247|000cef|            return watchExpressions.first(where: { $0.expression == trimmed })
248|5f3077|        }
249|adc83b|
250|5dc639|        let watch = WatchExpression(expression: trimmed, value: initialValue)
251|c52964|        var watches = watchExpressions
252|edd007|        watches.append(watch)
253|6dfccd|        watchExpressions = watches
254|8e207e|        return watch
255|27e597|    }
256|adc83b|
257|2f15d9|    func removeWatchExpression(id: WatchExpression.ID) {
258|c52964|        var watches = watchExpressions
259|0049f1|        watches.removeAll { $0.id == id }
260|6dfccd|        watchExpressions = watches
261|27e597|    }
262|adc83b|
263|4ab657|    func updateWatchExpression(id: WatchExpression.ID, expression: String) {
264|074c5b|        let trimmed = expression.trimmingCharacters(in: .whitespacesAndNewlines)
265|0eedd3|        guard !trimmed.isEmpty else { return }
266|8f9568|        guard let idx = watchExpressions.firstIndex(where: { $0.id == id }) else { return }
267|adc83b|
268|c52964|        var watches = watchExpressions
269|4d1038|        watches[idx].expression = trimmed
270|6dfccd|        watchExpressions = watches
271|27e597|    }
272|adc83b|
273|491aa0|    func setWatchValue(id: WatchExpression.ID, value: String) {
274|8f9568|        guard let idx = watchExpressions.firstIndex(where: { $0.id == id }) else { return }
275|c52964|        var watches = watchExpressions
276|017183|        watches[idx].value = value
277|6dfccd|        watchExpressions = watches
278|27e597|    }
